## Conflict / Question / Contradiction Protocol

Three append-only JSONL channels at `working/wiki/pass2-buckets/`. Always append; never overwrite.

### `questions-for-matt.jsonl` — when human input is needed

Use when:
- The candidate represents a relationship not in the locked vocabulary AND you think it should be (file a `vocabulary-gap` question with ≥3 example sentences).
- The candidate is genuinely undecidable across all four decision types (file a `prose-edge-other` question).
- The source or target node file is missing or unparseable (file an `infrastructure` question).

Schema:
```json
{"question_id": "q-<UTC-DATE>-<bucket-slug>-NNN", "bucket_id": "<bucket-id>", "agent": "prose-edge-classifier", "type": "vocabulary-gap|prose-edge-other|infrastructure", "text": "<one paragraph>", "context": {"source": "...", "target": "...", "snippet": "..."}, "blocking": false, "asked_at": "<UTC ISO8601>", "resolved_at": null, "resolution": null}
```

### `pass1-contradictions.jsonl` — when wiki contradicts a chapter extraction

If your reading of the source prose contradicts what Pass 1 chapter extractions said about the same entity, file a row. The `contradiction-surfacer` agent will pick it up. Don't try to resolve the contradiction yourself.

### `conflicts.jsonl` — when two prose snippets disagree about the same edge

If candidate A says edge X→Y is `SPOUSE_OF` and candidate B (different snippet) says X→Y is `LOVER_OF`, file a conflict row. Emit your best-judgment edge in `prose-edges/`, but log the conflict.

## Definition of Done — per input file

You exit successfully for an input candidates file when:
- Every candidate row in the input has a corresponding decision row in the matching output file (or candidate count was zero, in which case no output file is created).
- Every decision row carries the `candidate_kind` discriminator (matching the input).
- All emit_edge decisions use only the locked master vocabulary (~163 edge types in architecture.md § "Edge Types (Relationship Categories)").
- For `comention` emit_edge decisions: a direction was picked (`a_to_b`, `b_to_a`, or `symmetric`).
- All structured-channel rows you wanted to file are appended.
- You produced no output anywhere outside the input's matching `prose-edges/` sibling dir and the three append-only channels.

The launcher then runs `wiki-pass2-promote-prose-edges.py`, which reads your JSONL and appends accepted edges to nodes under a `## Edges (prose-derived)` subheading — keeping infobox edges immutable. For comention edges, the promoter writes the edge into the `source` node (per the direction the classifier picked) and adds an `evidence_chapter:` citation to the qualifier. You do not perform that promotion.

[Jon](wiki:Jon_Snow) sends [Alliser Thorne](wiki:Alliser_Thorne) out with [Dywen](wiki:Dywen) and one other man to range [beyond the Wall](wiki:Beyond_the_Wall). Thorne believes that Jon is sending him out to die, though Dywen mockingly says that they will teach him to be a true ranger. Thorne tells Jon that he will not refuse and give him the pleasure of cutting his head off as he did with [Janos Slynt](wiki:Janos_Slynt). Jon is also sending out two other rangings, one led by [Black Jack Bulwer](wiki:Jack_Bulwer) and another by [Kedge Whiteye](wiki:Kedge_Whiteye).

Jon descends the Wall with [Dolorous Edd](wiki:Eddison_Tollett), noting that he is receiving messages of need for more men from some of his commanders as more sightings of wildlings are being reported. [Rattleshirt](wiki:Rattleshirt) sees them and tells Jon that he should fight a man and not boys and they begin to spar. Rattleshirt is getting the best of him, despite fighting with a larger greatsword and no shield. Eventually they lose their swords, roll, and the fight ends with Rattleshirt on top. He is dragged off and Jon remarks that Rattleshirt fought well.

A raven arrives with a message(wiki:A_Dance_with_Dragons-Chapter_28.cite_ref-1) that Jon's half-sister [Arya](wiki:Arya_Stark) is to marry [Ramsay Bolton](wiki:Ramsay_Snow). The others ask how old his sister is, and he replies that she is eleven and that he has no sisters, only brothers in the Night's Watch. Jon later remembers his sister and the sword [Needle](wiki:Needle) he once gave to her, wondering if she still has it.

He goes for a walk and sees [Melisandre](wiki:Melisandre), though at first he believes her to be [Ygritte](wiki:Ygritte). Melisandre tells him that he should not worry over matters of the heart and that she sees that his sister will flee this marriage and ride towards them on a dying horse. She also foresees that three of the rangers sent out will die, and come back eyeless. As she is leaving she tells him that when the dead men return, he should take her hand and she will save his sister.

He passes his time chatting to the guards, gaining a few favors in the form of candles, books, paper, and a quill, as well as learning a little more about the general history of the [north](wiki:North). Davos writes a few letters to his sons to be delivered upon his death. It is then that [Robett Glover](wiki:Robett_Glover) appears at his door and politely asks him to follow; Davos is wary but notes his mannerisms and follows.
27	
28	Davos is led through secret hallways always going in different directions, passing the [old godswood](wiki:Godswood_of_White_Harbor) on the way. Eventually they end up in a room of the [New Castle](wiki:New_Castle) where Lord [Wyman Manderly](wiki:Wyman_Manderly) is waiting for him. Davos declines the offers of food and [wine](wiki:Wine); Wyman though, apologizes for his poor treatment of Davos. Wyman tells him that they must talk fast, as the [Freys](wiki:House_Frey) wait above, and Davos can hear the sounds of music and chatter above. Lord Wyman says that they must act quickly for Davos is "dead" and his head sits upon the Seal Gate. A criminal similar in appearance to Davos was executed in his place, his head and hands (with fingers carefully shortened) mounted on the wall. Davos learns that Wyman only plays along with the Freys because he has to, as his son [Wylis Manderly](wiki:Wylis_Manderly) is being held hostage, though he drinks for his safe return.(wiki:A_Dance_with_Dragons-Chapter_29.cite_ref-1)
29	
30	Davos says he was quite convinced by Wyman's daughters' behavior, though he was confused by [Wylla Manderly](wiki:Wylla_Manderly). Wyman responds that his young granddaughter was extremely brave to have said those things. There was truth in her words as the debt the [Manderlys](wiki:House_Manderly) owe the [Starks](wiki:House_Stark) and [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) can never be repaid. He adds, though, that he cannot show any sign of contempt to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) for fear of the harm they might do to his son. [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) told Wyman that he must confess his treason, yield White Harbor, declare his loyalty to King [Tommen](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon), and bend his knee to [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton). If he refused, Wylis would be killed as a traitor, White Harbor would be sacked, and his people would "suffer the same fates as the [Reynes](wiki:House_Reyne) of [Castamere](wiki:Castamere)". If he complies, his son will be released. By "killing" Davos, Wyman has proved his loyalty to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) and assured the release of Wylis, which Davos notes is a brave move.
31	
32	Wyman adds that he has been unable to send Stannis more information in his letters because the Freys infest his court, looking for any sign of treachery and that his [maester](wiki:Maester), [Theomore](wiki:Theomore), is [a distant cousin to the Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister_of_Lannisport), so Wyman does not trust him. Lord Manderly goes on to add his rage at his son [Wendel Manderly](wiki:Wendel_Manderly)'s death at the hands of the Freys, calling it murder and the Freys' claims fables. He tells Davos that the [north](wiki:North) remembers and his son is home, meaning that his act for the Freys is almost over as well.

Davos is told by Robett that Theon was captured at Winterfell, and is currently being flayed by the Boltons. He adds that all the women were also captured and marched away. Davos listens to the horrible things that [Ramsay Bolton](wiki:Ramsay_Snow) does to the captured women, sending them naked into the woods with half a day's head start before hunting them down with his dogs. Every so often, according to Wyman, some wench lives to tell the tale, but most are not as lucky. If caught, they are raped, flayed and their corpses fed to his dogs, while their skins adorn the [Dreadfort](wiki:Dreadfort). If Ramsay considered them good sport, he slits their throats before he flays them, otherwise it is the other way around.
35

He also tells Davos that Roose lies and played a darker part in the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding) than he has admitted. In addition, Davos is told that the Boltons and Freys know the [northmen](wiki:Northmen) are not expected to believe their lies but will be forced to accept them or else die as well.
37

## Narrative Arc

[Davos](wiki:Davos_Seaworth), still imprisoned in the [Wolf's Den](wiki:Wolf's_Den), is awaiting his death and hopes that he will at least die honorably, losing his head first and not his hands. He passes his time chatting to the guards, gaining a few favors in the form of candles, books, paper, and a quill, as well as learning a little more about the general history of the [north](wiki:North). Davos writes a few letters to his sons to be delivered upon his death. It is then that [Robett Glover](wiki:Robett_Glover) appears at his door and politely asks him to follow; Davos is wary but notes his mannerisms and follows.

Davos is led through secret hallways always going in different directions, passing the [old godswood](wiki:Godswood_of_White_Harbor) on the way. Eventually they end up in a room of the [New Castle](wiki:New_Castle) where Lord [Wyman Manderly](wiki:Wyman_Manderly) is waiting for him. Davos declines the offers of food and [wine](wiki:Wine); Wyman though, apologizes for his poor treatment of Davos. Wyman tells him that they must talk fast, as the [Freys](wiki:House_Frey) wait above, and Davos can hear the sounds of music and chatter above. Lord Wyman says that they must act quickly for Davos is "dead" and his head sits upon the Seal Gate. A criminal similar in appearance to Davos was executed in his place, his head and hands (with fingers carefully shortened) mounted on the wall. Davos learns that Wyman only plays along with the Freys because he has to, as his son [Wylis Manderly](wiki:Wylis_Manderly) is being held hostage, though he drinks for his safe return.(wiki:A_Dance_with_Dragons-Chapter_29.cite_ref-1)

Davos says he was quite convinced by Wyman's daughters' behavior, though he was confused by [Wylla Manderly](wiki:Wylla_Manderly). Wyman responds that his young granddaughter was extremely brave to have said those things. There was truth in her words as the debt the [Manderlys](wiki:House_Manderly) owe the [Starks](wiki:House_Stark) and [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) can never be repaid. He adds, though, that he cannot show any sign of contempt to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) for fear of the harm they might do to his son. [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister) told Wyman that he must confess his treason, yield White Harbor, declare his loyalty to King [Tommen](wiki:Tommen_Baratheon), and bend his knee to [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton). If he refused, Wylis would be killed as a traitor, White Harbor would be sacked, and his people would "suffer the same fates as the [Reynes](wiki:House_Reyne) of [Castamere](wiki:Castamere)". If he complies, his son will be released. By "killing" Davos, Wyman has proved his loyalty to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) and assured the release of Wylis, which Davos notes is a brave move.

Wyman adds that he has been unable to send Stannis more information in his letters because the Freys infest his court, looking for any sign of treachery and that his [maester](wiki:Maester), [Theomore](wiki:Theomore), is [a distant cousin to the Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister_of_Lannisport), so Wyman does not trust him. Lord Manderly goes on to add his rage at his son [Wendel Manderly](wiki:Wendel_Manderly)'s death at the hands of the Freys, calling it murder and the Freys' claims fables. He tells Davos that the [north](wiki:North) remembers and his son is home, meaning that his act for the Freys is almost over as well.

Wyman summons [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy)'s mute squire [Wex](wiki:Wex_Pyke), who survived the [sack of Winterfell](wiki:Sack_of_Winterfell). Davos listens to the horrible things that [Ramsay Bolton](wiki:Ramsay_Snow) does to the captured women, sending them naked into the woods with half a day's head start before hunting them down with his dogs. Every so often, according to Wyman, some wench lives to tell the tale, but most are not as lucky. If caught, they are raped, flayed and their corpses fed to his dogs, while their skins adorn the [Dreadfort](wiki:Dreadfort). If Ramsay considered them good sport, he slits their throats before he flays them, otherwise it is the other way around.

Robett tells a stunned Davos that the evil is in Ramsay's blood, sired from rape. Wyman adds that the Freys are no better than Ramsay. He also tells Davos that Roose lies and played a darker part in the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding) than he has admitted. In addition, Davos is told that the Boltons and Freys know the [northmen](wiki:Northmen) are not expected to believe their lies but will be forced to accept them or else die as well.

Wyman tells Davos about the mighty fleet he has built up, which Davos saw when he entered the port. ## Narrative Arc

[Daenerys](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) begins every morning by counting the enemy ships in the harbour. Today she has counted roughly twenty-five. She has an argument with her admiral [Groleo](wiki:Groleo), who is advising her to send her dragons to attack the enemy fleet. Daenerys tells him to attack with ships, but he replies that she dismantled his ships, and all the timber in twenty leagues has been burned so he can build no more.

[Hizdahr](wiki:Hizdahr_zo_Loraq) has thus far had success in preventing further murders. She has had her betrothed followed and [Skahaz mo Kandaq](wiki:Skahaz_mo_Kandaq) reports that Hizdahr has visited eleven pyramids and it has been twenty-six days since a murder has been attempted. He surmises that he must be one of the [Sons of the Harpy](wiki:Sons_of_the_Harpy) or else these others would not have listened to his words alone. Skahaz asks that he may question Hizdahr, but Dany will not let him, stating that she must trust in Hizdahr or face open rebellion in the city. Skahaz also advises her that the fleets in the blockade all have family members in [Meereen](wiki:Meereen) and that she should use them as hostages, a contemplation she also rejects. [Barristan Selmy](wiki:Barristan_Selmy) advises her that she has done a wise thing.

[Grey Worm](wiki:Grey_Worm) meets with her next to tell her that the first refugees from [Astapor](wiki:Astapor) are arriving in Meereen and that Astapor is burning. Dany learns of [the fall of Astapor](wiki:Siege_of_Astapor), and that many inhabitants have contracted the [bloody flux](wiki:Bloody_flux) while under siege, with the survivors now fleeing towards Meereen.(wiki:A_Dance_with_Dragons-Chapter_30.cite_ref-Radwd30pages_398-402.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-0)(wiki:A_Dance_with_Dragons-Chapter_30.cite_ref-2) The survivors also say that they believed and waited everyday for Daenerys to come and save them. There are further appeals to use the dragons, however they are followed with comments that they cannot be controlled right now, and even the [Unsullied](wiki:Unsullied) are afraid to go into the dragon pit to feed them. Dany says she cannot and will not unleash the dragons at this point in time. Ultimately she decides not to let the Astapori into the city and that they should have a separate camp outside the city until the flux passes.

Ser Barristan appeals to her to take the battle to the enemy. She then reminds herself that a queen represents her people and not just her own person. She then says that she must marry Hizdahr, for the good of the city.

## Identity

A Dance with Dragons-Chapter 31 is a meta.chapter from the AWOIAF wiki.

## Edges


## Narrative Arc

[Melisandre](wiki:Melisandre) is in her chambers, looking into the fire and trying to receive her visions. When she looks for Stannis, she sees eyeless faces staring out, crying blood. Then she sees towers by the sea followed by skulls rising out of the ocean turning to mist and great winged beasts flying in the sky. She tries to see the girl on the fleeing horse again as she knows [Jon](wiki:Jon_Snow) will want to know more, but she cannot find anything. She does see that there is death around Jon and that dangers and enemies lurk all around him.

When [Devan](wiki:Devan_Seaworth) asks her what she sees, she replies with her usual answer "Much and more." She does not let him know how difficult it is or how much pain she endures to try and see these things, for fear of letting others know that this task is not as simple as she lets on. Melisandre knows and sees that [Davos](wiki:Davos_Seaworth) is the most loyal to [Stannis](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) thus she has kept Devan back against the young boy's wishes. She knows that Davos has lost too many sons already and although Stannis has ridden out, she keeps the boy in order to save one of Davos' sons.

Melisandre does not sleep much, often just an hour a night, as she fears to dream and what she sees there, re-occurring images of the words "Lot Seven" and "Melony".

She sends Devan to go get [Rattleshirt](wiki:Rattleshirt) and bring him to her. She speaks with him and tells him to keep his bones on, in order to keep the [glamor](wiki:Glamor) spell active. She also asks that he go and fetch the girl she saw on the dying horse. She tells him of her vision and asks him where the girl might be coming up, and, knowing the North well, he can guess her location, west of [Long Lake](wiki:Long_Lake). Their talk is disrupted by the single long drone of a horn, marking the return of the rangers. She tells Rattleshirt to wait for him there and she goes to see the rangers, thinking of her prediction of three dead rangers.

Upon arrival she sees Jon with the heads of [Black Jack Bulwer](wiki:Black_Jack_Bulwer), [Hairy Hal](wiki:Hairy_Hal) and [Garth Greyfeather](wiki:Garth_Greyfeather), impaled on spears.(wiki:A_Dance_with_Dragons-Chapter_31.cite_ref-1) She walks with Jon and they go back to her room. There he sees Rattleshirt, but she removes the glamor to show him that he is actually [Mance Rayder](wiki:Mance_Rayder), whom she has spared. She tells him that he will bring Jon back his sister, but Jon mistrusts her. She does not reveal the difficulty of her glamor spell to the men, thinking to herself that the more effortless sorcery appears, the more men fear it and her.
